title: Microlobius foetidus
taxonomy: Microlobius foetidus belongs to the family Fabaceae. This plant is part of the subfamily Faboideae. Further classification places it in the tribe Genisteae. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The exact genus classification of Microlobius foetidus is disputed in some botanical sources, with some considering it to be a synonym of Dalea.

intro:
I couldn't find any information about the plant Microlobius foetidus. However, based on available research, I can suggest that the plant Microlobia foetida (also known as  'Stink Bush') is a plant species in the Fabaceae family.

Microlobia foetida is a small shrub native to tropical Africa, characterized by its unpleasant odor. The health benefits of this plant are attributed to its antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which may aid in treating infections and wounds.
